message:
fix: show timeout fallback when card loading exceeds 30 seconds (#3253) (#3314)

When a card's child component reports isLoading:true but never transitions
out (due to network failure, interrupted render, or data source
unavailability), the card could remain stuck in a loading state
indefinitely.

The existing cardLoadingTimedOut guard already forced the card out of the
loading state after CARD_LOADING_TIMEOUT_MS (30s), but it only showed a
"Refresh failed" badge in the header — the content area was blank or
showed a broken child component.

This commit adds:
- A visible "Loading timed out" fallback UI in the card content area with
  a warning icon, explanation message, and retry button
- The child component is kept mounted (hidden) so it can resume if data
  eventually arrives
- effectiveConsecutiveFailures is set to 1 when timeout fires, so the
  failure badge displays a meaningful count
- i18n keys for the timeout message in all 9 locale files

Signed-off-by: Andy <andy@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: andy anderson <andy@andys-MacBook-Pro.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
